_comment: Blob proof request schema (§2.2). K ≥ 1 EIP-4844 blobs + N execution proofs tiling their combined block range. Emits the 14-field PI tuple defined in §2.4.
_comment_chainId: Chain identifier (uint64). Needed for sender recovery during DA truncation (§2.2 step 1). Same value across all execution proofs in this blob; committed transitively via their `dynamicChainConfigHash` PI field.
_comment_blockRange: Combined block range across all K blobs. Equals `blobs[0].blockRange.startBlockNumber .. blobs[K-1].blockRange.endBlockNumber`; consecutive blob ranges must be contiguous.
_comment_blobs: K = 2 blobs in this example (K = 1 is the degenerate single-blob case). Ordered by on-chain shnarf-chain order. Each entry carries KZG inputs (no compressed payload — see below), its block range, and `blockRlps` — the canonical full block RLPs (one per block in [startBlockNumber, endBlockNumber]), same canonical form the execution proof receives. The guest truncates each block internally per §3.2, RLP-encodes the truncated form, LZ4-compresses it, zero-pads to BLOB_BYTES_LENGTH (4096 × 32 = 131072 bytes), and verifies the result against `blobKzgCommitment`/`blobKzgProof` via `verify_blob_kzg_proof` (§2.2 step 1). The compressed blob bytes are *not* a witness field — they are recomputed inside the guest; the KZG check accepts iff those computed bytes match what the sequencer committed to on L1, so a separate byte-equality assertion would be redundant. There is no separately witnessed truncated form either; the full block RLPs' authenticity is anchored by KZG plus downstream cross-checks against the execution proofs (block-hash boundaries, txFromsHash).

blockRlps: _comment: m_b = 10 entries (one canonical block RLP per block in the range); abbreviated

_comment_shnarfTransition: parentShnarf is the inbound shnarf before blob[0]; endShnarf is the outbound shnarf after blob[K-1]. Intermediate shnarfs are not exposed.
_comment_executionProofs: N execution proofs tiling the combined block range, in canonical (start..end) order. Each entry inlines everything the blob-proof guest needs for that execution proof: the recursive STARK `proof` bytes, the 15-field `publicInputs` tuple, and the preimages of the three hash-committed PI fields — `L2L1MsgList` (preimage of publicInputs.L2L1MessagesHash), `froms` (preimage of publicInputs.txFromsHash), and `addrs` (preimage of publicInputs.filteredAddressesHash, §6.5).

executionProofs._comment: remaining N-1 entries (covering 1000504-1000510, 1000511-1000515, 1000516-1000520) have the same shape; abbreviated in the fixture
